steps.title: Focus on duration
steps.content: The goal is not just to mention that Rhinebeck and Larreynaga are sister cities. It is to emphasize how long their relationship has lasted. The key note is the start date, 1989, and the sentence should show that the partnership has continued from then to the present. A sentence that only names the date as background would be less effective because it would not stress duration.

steps.title: Eliminate weak time frames
steps.content:
• Choice A uses World War II to discuss sister cities generally, not this specific pair.
  • Choice C mentions 1989 but shifts the focus to Larreynaga's location.
  • Choice D discusses distance and comparison with other sister cities without giving any timeframe.

steps.title: Use the ongoing relationship
steps.content: Choice B says Rhinebeck and Larreynaga have engaged in cultural exchange and partnership since 1989. That wording uses the start date to stress an ongoing relationship, which directly matches the student's goal of emphasizing duration across many years of sister-city connection. The answer is B.
